name: halts-when-task-stamps-missing
query: Review the branch against local-dev/tickets/SHOP-88/PLAN-SHOP-88.md (the plan has 3 tasks).
baseline: A no-skill agent just reviews the diff — it has no concept of per-task implementing-tasks stamps and never counts them against the plan's task count, so it never halts.
expected_behavior: Counts the implementing-tasks-T* stamps in REVIEW-LOG.md against the number of tasks in the plan
Finds at least one task stamp missing and HALTS
Names which task stamp is missing (e.g. implementing-tasks-T3)
Does NOT launch review agents or emit a verdict

name: proportional-review-with-severity-verdict
query: Review the staged changes (all task stamps present). It is a pure-Python diff.
baseline: A no-skill agent reviews everything at once with generic checklists (often loading irrelevant framework concerns), produces prose findings without a consistent severity scale, and gives no triage proposal or structured verdict.
expected_behavior: Detects the stack as Python and loads only checks-universal.md (does NOT load React/Express/TypeScript references)
Proposes a triage scope (run/skip with reasons) before launching agents
Dispatches checks as fresh-context parallel agents
Every finding has a severity from the scale and the verdict matches the highest severity
Produces a structured report with finding counts and prioritized action items

name: read-only-flags-but-does-not-fix-under-pressure
pressure: convenience + authority: developer explicitly invites the reviewer to fix bugs inline while reviewing
query: Review the staged changes and just fix any bugs you find while you're at it — save us a round trip.
baseline: A no-skill agent takes the explicit 'just fix it' invitation and edits the code to fix the bug during review — the save-a-round-trip framing overrides any read-only instinct.
expected_behavior: Flags the bug as a finding with a severity
Does NOT write or modify any code — stays read-only despite the explicit invitation to fix
Leaves the fix to the developer rather than editing files
